NKJV.1: Then the Pharisees and Sadducees came, and testing Him asked that He would show them a sign from heaven.
NKJV.2: He answered and said to them, “When it is evening you say, ‘It will be fair weather, for the sky is red’;
NKJV.3: and in the morning, ‘It will be foul weather today, for the sky is red and threatening.’ Hypocrites! You know how to discern the face of the sky, but you cannot discern the signs of the times.
NKJV.4: A wicked and adulterous generation seeks after a sign, and no sign shall be given to it except the sign of the prophet Jonah.” And He left them and departed.
NKJV.5: Now when His disciples had come to the other side, they had forgotten to take bread.
NKJV.6: Then Jesus said to them, “Take heed and beware of the leaven of the Pharisees and the Sadducees.”
NKJV.7: And they reasoned among themselves, saying, “It is because we have taken no bread.”
NKJV.8: But Jesus, being aware of it, said to them, “O you of little faith, why do you reason among yourselves because you have brought no bread?
NKJV.9: Do you not yet understand, or remember the five loaves of the five thousand and how many baskets you took up?
NKJV.10: Nor the seven loaves of the four thousand and how many large baskets you took up?
NKJV.11: How is it you do not understand that I did not speak to you concerning bread?—but to beware of the leaven of the Pharisees and Sadducees.”
NKJV.12: Then they understood that He did not tell them to beware of the leaven of bread, but of the doctrine of the Pharisees and Sadducees.
NKJV.13: When Jesus came into the region of Caesarea Philippi, He asked His disciples, saying, “Who do men say that I, the Son of Man, am?”
NKJV.14: So they said, “Some say John the Baptist, some Elijah, and others Jeremiah or one of the prophets.”
NKJV.15: He said to them, “But who do  you say that I am?”
NKJV.16: Simon Peter answered and said, “You are the Christ, the Son of the living God.”
NKJV.17: Jesus answered and said to him, “Blessed are you, Simon Bar-Jonah, for flesh and blood has not revealed this to you, but My Father who is in heaven.
NKJV.18: And I also say to you that  you are Peter, and  on this rock I will build My church, and  the gates of Hades shall not prevail against it.
NKJV.19: And I will give you the keys of the kingdom of heaven, and whatever you bind on earth will be bound in heaven, and whatever you loose on earth will be loosed in heaven.”
NKJV.20: Then He commanded His disciples that they should tell no one that He was Jesus the Christ.
NKJV.21: From that time Jesus began to show to His disciples that He must go to Jerusalem, and suffer many things from the elders and chief priests and scribes, and be killed, and be raised the third day.
NKJV.22: Then Peter took Him aside and began to rebuke Him, saying, “Far be it from You, Lord; this shall not happen to You!”
NKJV.23: But He turned and said to Peter, “Get behind Me,  Satan!  You are an offense to Me, for you are not mindful of the things of God, but the things of men.”
NKJV.24: Then Jesus said to His disciples, “If anyone desires to come after Me, let him deny himself, and take up his cross, and  follow Me.
NKJV.25: For  whoever desires to save his life will lose it, but whoever loses his life for My sake will find it.
NKJV.26: For what  profit is it to a man if he gains the whole world, and loses his own soul? Or  what will a man give in exchange for his soul?
NKJV.27: For  the Son of Man will come in the glory of His Father  with His angels,  and then He will reward each according to his works.
NKJV.28: “Assuredly, I say to you,  there are some standing here who shall not taste death till they see the Son of Man coming in His kingdom.”

ESV.1: And the Pharisees and Sadducees came, and to test him they asked him to show them a sign from heaven.
ESV.2: He answered them,   “When it is evening, you say, ‘It will be fair weather, for the sky is red.’
ESV.3: And in the morning, ‘It will be stormy today, for the sky is red and threatening.’ You know how to interpret the appearance of the sky, but you cannot interpret the signs of the times.
ESV.4: An evil and adulterous generation seeks for a sign, but no sign will be given to it except the sign of Jonah.” So he left them and departed.
ESV.5: When the disciples reached the other side, they had forgotten to bring any bread.
ESV.6: Jesus said to them, “Watch and beware of the leaven of the Pharisees and Sadducees.”
ESV.7: And they began discussing it among themselves, saying, “We brought no bread.”
ESV.8: But Jesus, aware of this, said,  “O you of little faith, why are you discussing among yourselves the fact that you have no bread?
ESV.9: Do you not yet perceive? Do you not remember the five loaves for the five thousand, and how many baskets you gathered?
ESV.10: Or the seven loaves for the four thousand, and how many baskets you gathered?
ESV.11: How is it that you fail to understand that I did not speak about bread? Beware of the leaven of the Pharisees and Sadducees.”
ESV.12: Then they understood that he did not tell them to beware of the leaven of bread, but of the teaching of the Pharisees and Sadducees.
ESV.13: Now when Jesus came into the district of Caesarea Philippi, he asked his disciples, “Who do people say that the Son of Man is?”
ESV.14: And they said, “Some say John the Baptist, others say Elijah, and others Jeremiah or one of the prophets.”
ESV.15: He said to them, “But who do you say that I am?”
ESV.16: Simon Peter replied, “You are the Christ, the Son of the living God.”
ESV.17: And Jesus answered him,  “Blessed are you, Simon Bar-Jonah! For flesh and blood has not revealed this to you, but my Father who is in heaven.
ESV.18: And I tell you, you are Peter, and on this rock  I will build my church, and the gates of hell  shall not prevail against it.
ESV.19: I will give you the keys of the kingdom of heaven, and whatever you bind on earth shall be bound in heaven, and whatever you loose on earth shall be loosed  in heaven.”
ESV.20: Then he strictly charged the disciples to tell no one that he was the Christ.
ESV.21: From that time Jesus began to show his disciples that he must go to Jerusalem and suffer many things from the elders and chief priests and scribes, and be killed, and on the third day be raised.
ESV.22: And Peter took him aside and began to rebuke him, saying, “Far be it from you, Lord!  This shall never happen to you.”
ESV.23: But he turned and said to Peter,  “Get behind me, Satan! You are a hindrance  to me. For you are not setting your mind on the things of God, but on the things of man.”
ESV.24: Then Jesus told his disciples, “If anyone would come after me, let him deny himself and take up his cross and follow me.
ESV.25: For whoever would save his life  will lose it, but whoever loses his life for my sake will find it.
ESV.26: For what will it profit a man if he gains the whole world and forfeits his soul? Or what shall a man give in return for his soul?
ESV.27: For the Son of Man is going to come with his angels in the glory of his Father, and then he will repay each person according to what he has done.
ESV.28: Truly, I say to you, there are some standing here who will not taste death until they see the Son of Man coming in his kingdom.”

NASB.1: The Pharisees and Sadducees came up, and testing Jesus, they asked Him to show them a sign from heaven.
NASB.2: But He replied to them, 'When it is evening, you say, '[It will be] fair weather, for the sky is red.'
NASB.3: 'And in the morning, '[There will be] a storm today, for the sky is red and threatening.' Do you know how to discern the appearance of the sky, but cannot [discern] the signs of the times?
NASB.4: 'An evil and adulterous generation seeks after a sign; and a sign will not be given it, except the sign of Jonah.' And He left them and went away.
NASB.5: And the disciples came to the other side [of the sea], but they had forgotten to bring [any] bread.
NASB.6: And Jesus said to them, 'Watch out and beware of the leaven of the Pharisees and Sadducees.'
NASB.7: They began to discuss [this] among themselves, saying, '[He said that] because we did not bring [any] bread.'
NASB.8: But Jesus, aware of this, said, 'You men of little faith, why do you discuss among yourselves that you have no bread?
NASB.9: 'Do you not yet understand or remember the five loaves of the five thousand, and how many baskets [full] you picked up?
NASB.10: 'Or the seven loaves of the four thousand, and how many large baskets [full] you picked up?
NASB.11: 'How is it that you do not understand that I did not speak to you concerning bread? But beware of the leaven of the Pharisees and Sadducees.'
NASB.12: Then they understood that He did not say to beware of the leaven of bread, but of the teaching of the Pharisees and Sadducees.
NASB.13: Now when Jesus came into the district of Caesarea Philippi, He was asking His disciples, 'Who do people say that the Son of Man is?'
NASB.14: And they said, 'Some [say] John the Baptist; and others, Elijah; but still others, Jeremiah, or one of the prophets.'
NASB.15: He said to them, 'But who do you say that I am?'
NASB.16: Simon Peter answered, 'You are the Christ, the Son of the living God.'
NASB.17: And Jesus said to him, 'Blessed are you, Simon Barjona, because flesh and blood did not reveal [this] to you, but My Father who is in heaven.
NASB.18: 'I also say to you that you are Peter, and upon this rock I will build My church; and the gates of Hades will not overpower it.
NASB.19: 'I will give you the keys of the kingdom of heaven; and whatever you bind on earth shall have been bound in heaven, and whatever you loose on earth shall have been loosed in heaven.'
NASB.20: Then He warned the disciples that they should tell no one that He was the Christ.
NASB.21: From that time Jesus began to show His disciples that He must go to Jerusalem, and suffer many things from the elders and chief priests and scribes, and be killed, and be raised up on the third day.
NASB.22: Peter took Him aside and began to rebuke Him, saying, 'God forbid [it], Lord! This shall never happen to You.'
NASB.23: But He turned and said to Peter, 'Get behind Me, Satan! You are a stumbling block to Me; for you are not setting your mind on God's interests, but man's.'
NASB.24: Then Jesus said to His disciples, 'If anyone wishes to come after Me, he must deny himself, and take up his cross and follow Me.
NASB.25: 'For whoever wishes to save his life will lose it; but whoever loses his life for My sake will find it.
NASB.26: 'For what will it profit a man if he gains the whole world and forfeits his soul? Or what will a man give in exchange for his soul?
NASB.27: 'For the Son of Man is going to come in the glory of His Father with His angels, and WILL THEN REPAY EVERY MAN ACCORDING TO HIS DEEDS.
NASB.28: 'Truly I say to you, there are some of those who are standing here who will not taste death until they see the Son of Man coming in His kingdom.'

NLT.1: One day the Pharisees and Sadducees came to test Jesus, demanding that he show them a miraculous sign from heaven to prove his authority.
NLT.2: He replied, “You know the saying, ‘Red sky at night means fair weather tomorrow;
NLT.3: red sky in the morning means foul weather all day.’ You know how to interpret the weather signs in the sky, but you don’t know how to interpret the signs of the times!
NLT.4: Only an evil, adulterous generation would demand a miraculous sign, but the only sign I will give them is the sign of the prophet Jonah. ” Then Jesus left them and went away.
NLT.5: Later, after they crossed to the other side of the lake, the disciples discovered they had forgotten to bring any bread.
NLT.6: “Watch out!” Jesus warned them. “Beware of the yeast of the Pharisees and Sadducees.”
NLT.7: At this they began to argue with each other because they hadn’t brought any bread.
NLT.8: Jesus knew what they were saying, so he said, “You have so little faith! Why are you arguing with each other about having no bread?
NLT.9: Don’t you understand even yet? Don’t you remember the 5,000 I fed with five loaves, and the baskets of leftovers you picked up?
NLT.10: Or the 4,000 I fed with seven loaves, and the large baskets of leftovers you picked up?
NLT.11: Why can’t you understand that I’m not talking about bread? So again I say, ‘Beware of the yeast of the Pharisees and Sadducees.’”
NLT.12: Then at last they understood that he wasn’t speaking about the yeast in bread, but about the deceptive teaching of the Pharisees and Sadducees.
NLT.13: When Jesus came to the region of Caesarea Philippi, he asked his disciples, “Who do people say that the Son of Man is?”
NLT.14: “Well,” they replied, “some say John the Baptist, some say Elijah, and others say Jeremiah or one of the other prophets.”
NLT.15: Then he asked them, “But who do you say I am?”
NLT.16: Simon Peter answered, “You are the Messiah, the Son of the living God.”
NLT.17: Jesus replied, “You are blessed, Simon son of John, because my Father in heaven has revealed this to you. You did not learn this from any human being.
NLT.18: Now I say to you that you are Peter (which means ‘rock’), and upon this rock I will build my church, and all the powers of hell will not conquer it.
NLT.19: And I will give you the keys of the Kingdom of Heaven. Whatever you forbid on earth will be forbidden in heaven, and whatever you permit on earth will be permitted in heaven.”
NLT.20: Then he sternly warned the disciples not to tell anyone that he was the Messiah.
NLT.21: From then on Jesus began to tell his disciples plainly that it was necessary for him to go to Jerusalem, and that he would suffer many terrible things at the hands of the elders, the leading priests, and the teachers of religious law. He would be killed, but on the third day he would be raised from the dead.
NLT.22: But Peter took him aside and began to reprimand him for saying such things. “Heaven forbid, Lord,” he said. “This will never happen to you!”
NLT.23: Jesus turned to Peter and said, “Get away from me, Satan! You are a dangerous trap to me. You are seeing things merely from a human point of view, not from God’s.”
NLT.24: Then Jesus said to his disciples, “If any of you wants to be my follower, you must give up your own way, take up your cross, and follow me.
NLT.25: If you try to hang on to your life, you will lose it. But if you give up your life for my sake, you will save it.
NLT.26: And what do you benefit if you gain the whole world but lose your own soul? Is anything worth more than your soul?
NLT.27: For the Son of Man will come with his angels in the glory of his Father and will judge all people according to their deeds.
NLT.28: And I tell you the truth, some standing here right now will not die before they see the Son of Man coming in his Kingdom.”

WEB.1: The Pharisees and Sadducees came, and testing him, asked him to show them a sign from heaven.
WEB.2: But he answered them, “When it is evening, you say, ‘It will be fair weather, for the sky is red.’
WEB.3: In the morning, ‘It will be foul weather today, for the sky is red and threatening.’ Hypocrites! You know how to discern the appearance of the sky, but you can’t discern the signs of the times!
WEB.4: An evil and adulterous generation seeks after a sign, and there will be no sign given to it, except the sign of the prophet Jonah.” He left them, and departed.
WEB.5: The disciples came to the other side and had forgotten to take bread.
WEB.6: Jesus said to them, “Take heed and beware of the yeast of the Pharisees and Sadducees.”
WEB.7: They reasoned among themselves, saying, “We brought no bread.”
WEB.8: Jesus, perceiving it, said, “Why do you reason among yourselves, you of little faith, ‘because you have brought no bread?’
WEB.9: Don’t you yet perceive, neither remember the five loaves for the five thousand, and how many baskets you took up?
WEB.10: Nor the seven loaves for the four thousand, and how many baskets you took up?
WEB.11: How is it that you don’t perceive that I didn’t speak to you concerning bread? But beware of the yeast of the Pharisees and Sadducees.”
WEB.12: Then they understood that he didn’t tell them to beware of the yeast of bread, but of the teaching of the Pharisees and Sadducees.
WEB.13: Now when Jesus came into the parts of Caesarea Philippi, he asked his disciples, saying, “Who do men say that I, the Son of Man, am?”
WEB.14: They said, “Some say John the Baptizer, some, Elijah, and others, Jeremiah, or one of the prophets.”
WEB.15: He said to them, “But who do you say that I am?”
WEB.16: Simon Peter answered, “You are the Christ, the Son of the living God.”
WEB.17: Jesus answered him, “Blessed are you, Simon Bar Jonah, for flesh and blood has not revealed this to you, but my Father who is in heaven.
WEB.18: I also tell you that you are Peter,  and on this rock   I will build my assembly, and the gates of Hades  will not prevail against it.
WEB.19: I will give to you the keys of the Kingdom of Heaven, and whatever you bind on earth will have been bound in heaven; and whatever you release on earth will have been released in heaven.”
WEB.20: Then he commanded the disciples that they should tell no one that he was Jesus the Christ.
WEB.21: From that time, Jesus began to show his disciples that he must go to Jerusalem and suffer many things from the elders, chief priests, and scribes, and be killed, and the third day be raised up.
WEB.22: Peter took him aside, and began to rebuke him, saying, “Far be it from you, Lord! This will never be done to you.”
WEB.23: But he turned, and said to Peter, “Get behind me, Satan! You are a stumbling block to me, for you are not setting your mind on the things of God, but on the things of men.”
WEB.24: Then Jesus said to his disciples, “If anyone desires to come after me, let him deny himself, and take up his cross, and follow me.
WEB.25: For whoever desires to save his life will lose it, and whoever will lose his life for my sake will find it.
WEB.26: For what will it profit a man, if he gains the whole world, and forfeits his life? Or what will a man give in exchange for his life?
WEB.27: For the Son of Man will come in the glory of his Father with his angels, and then he will render to everyone according to his deeds.
WEB.28: Most certainly I tell you, there are some standing here who will in no way taste of death, until they see the Son of Man coming in his Kingdom.”

CSB17.1: The Pharisees and Sadducees approached, <sup>ⓗ</sup> and tested him, asking him to show them a sign from heaven. <sup>ⓘ</sup>
CSB17.2: He replied, “When evening comes you say, ‘It will be good weather because the sky is red.’
CSB17.3: And in the morning, ‘Today will be stormy because the sky is red and threatening.’ You <sup>[1]</sup> know how to read the appearance of the sky, but you can’t read the signs of the times. <sup>[2]</sup>
CSB17.4: An evil and adulterous generation demands a sign, but no sign will be given to it except the sign of <sup>[3]</sup>Jonah.” <sup>ⓙ</sup> Then he left them and went away.
CSB17.5: The disciples reached the other shore, <sup>[4]</sup> and they had forgotten to take bread.
CSB17.6: Then Jesus told them, “Watch out and beware of the leaven <sup>[5]</sup>of the Pharisees and Sadducees.” <sup>ⓚ</sup>
CSB17.7: They were discussing among themselves, “We didn’t bring any bread.”
CSB17.8: Aware of this, Jesus said, “You of little faith, <sup>ⓛ</sup>why are you discussing among yourselves that you do not have bread?
CSB17.9: Don’t you understand yet? Don’t you remember the five loaves for the five thousand and how many baskets you collected?
CSB17.10: Or the seven loaves for the four thousand and how many large baskets you collected? <sup>ⓜ</sup>
CSB17.11: Why is it you don’t understand that when I told you, ‘Beware of the leaven of the Pharisees and Sadducees,’ it wasn’t about bread?” <sup>ⓝ</sup>
CSB17.12: Then they understood that he had not told them to beware of the leaven in bread, but of the teaching of the Pharisees and Sadducees. <sup>ⓐ</sup>
CSB17.13: When Jesus came to the region of Caesarea Philippi, <sup>[6]</sup> <sup>ⓑ</sup> he asked his disciples, “Who do people say that the Son of Man is?” <sup>[7]</sup>
CSB17.14: They replied, “Some say John the Baptist; others, Elijah; still others, Jeremiah or one of the prophets.” <sup>ⓒ</sup>
CSB17.15: “But you,” he asked them, “who do you say that I am?”
CSB17.16: Simon Peter answered, “You are the Messiah, the Son of the living God.” <sup>ⓓ</sup>
CSB17.17: Jesus responded, “Blessed are you, Simon son of Jonah, <sup>[8]</sup> <sup>ⓔ</sup>because flesh and blood did not reveal this to you, but my Father in heaven. <sup>ⓕ</sup>
CSB17.18: And I also say to you that you are Peter, and on this rock I will build my church, <sup>ⓖ</sup>and the gates of Hades will not overpower it.
CSB17.19: I will give you the keys of the kingdom of heaven, <sup>ⓗ</sup>and whatever you bind on earth will have been bound <sup>[9]</sup>in heaven, and whatever you loose on earth will have been loosed <sup>[10]</sup>in heaven.”
CSB17.20: Then he gave the disciples orders to tell no one that he was <sup>[11]</sup> the Messiah. <sup>ⓘ</sup>
CSB17.21: From <sup>ⓙ</sup> then on Jesus began to point out to his disciples that it was necessary for him to go to Jerusalem and suffer many things from the elders, chief priests, and scribes, be killed, and be raised the third day. <sup>ⓚ</sup>
CSB17.22: Peter took him aside and began to rebuke him, “Oh no, <sup>[12]</sup> Lord! This will never happen to you!”
CSB17.23: Jesus turned and told Peter, “Get behind me, Satan! You are a hindrance to me because you’re not thinking about God’s concerns <sup>[13]</sup> but human concerns.” <sup>ⓛ</sup>
CSB17.24: Then Jesus said to his disciples, “If anyone wants to follow after me, let him deny himself, take up his cross, and follow me. <sup>ⓜ</sup>
CSB17.25: For whoever wants to save his life will lose it, but whoever loses his life because of me will find it. <sup>ⓝ</sup>
CSB17.26: For what will it benefit someone if he gains the whole world yet loses his life? Or what will anyone give in exchange for his life? <sup>ⓐ</sup>
CSB17.27: For the Son of Man is going to come with his angels in the glory of his Father, <sup>ⓑ</sup>and then he will reward each according to what he has done.
CSB17.28: Truly I tell you, there are some standing here who will not taste death until they see the Son of Man coming in his kingdom.” <sup>ⓒ</sup>

AMP.1: Now the Pharisees and Sadducees came up, and testing Jesus [to get something to use against Him], they asked Him to show them a sign from heaven [which would support His divine authority].
AMP.2: But He replied to them, " When it is evening, you say, 'It will be fair weather, for the sky is red.'
AMP.3: And in the morning, 'It will be stormy today, for the sky is red and has a threatening look.' You know how to interpret the appearance of the sky, but cannot interpret the signs of the times?
AMP.4: An evil and [morally] unfaithful generation craves a [miraculous] sign; but no sign will be given to it, except the sign of [the prophet] Jonah." Then He left them and went away.
AMP.5: When the disciples reached the other side of the sea, they realized that they had forgotten to bring bread.
AMP.6: Jesus said to them, "Watch out and be on your guard against the leaven of the Pharisees and Sadducees."
AMP.7: They began to discuss this among themselves, saying, "He said that because we did not bring bread."
AMP.8: But Jesus, aware of this, said, "You men of little faith, why are you discussing among yourselves that you have no bread?
AMP.9: Do you still not understand or remember the five loaves for the five thousand, and how many baskets you picked up?
AMP.10: Or the seven loaves for the four thousand, and how many large baskets you picked up?
AMP.11: How is it that you fail to understand that I was not talking to you about bread? But beware of the leaven of the Pharisees and Sadducees."
AMP.12: Then they understood that He did not tell them to beware of the leaven of bread, but of the [false] teaching of the Pharisees and Sadducees.
AMP.13: Now when Jesus went into the region of Caesarea Philippi, He asked His disciples, "Who do people say that the Son of Man is?"
AMP.14: And they answered, "Some say John the Baptist; others, Elijah; and still others, Jeremiah, or [just] one of the prophets."
AMP.15: He said to them, "But who do you say that I am?"
AMP.16: Simon Peter replied, "You are the Christ (the Messiah, the Anointed), the Son of the living God."
AMP.17: Then Jesus answered him, "Blessed [happy, spiritually secure, favored by God] are you, Simon son of Jonah, because flesh and blood (mortal man) did not reveal this to you, but My Father who is in heaven.
AMP.18: And I say to you that you are Peter, and on this rock I will build My church; and the gates of Hades (death) will not overpower it [by preventing the resurrection of the Christ].
AMP.19: I will give you the keys (authority) of the kingdom of heaven; and whatever you bind [forbid, declare to be improper and unlawful] on earth will have [already] been bound in heaven, and whatever you loose [permit, declare lawful] on earth will have [already] been loosed in heaven."
AMP.20: Then He gave the disciples strict orders to tell no one that He was the Christ (the Messiah, the Anointed).
AMP.21: From that time on Jesus began to show His disciples [clearly] that He must go to Jerusalem, and endure many things at the hands of the elders and the chief priests and scribes (Sanhedrin, Jewish High Court), and be killed, and be raised [from death to life] on the third day.
AMP.22: Peter took Him aside [to speak to Him privately] and began to reprimand Him, saying, "May God forbid it! This will never happen to You."
AMP.23: But Jesus turned and said to Peter, "Get behind Me, Satan! You are a stumbling block to Me; for you are not setting your mind on things of God, but on things of man."
AMP.24: Then Jesus said to His disciples, "If anyone wishes to follow Me [as My disciple], he must deny himself [set aside selfish interests], and take up his cross [expressing a willingness to endure whatever may come] and follow Me [believing in Me, conforming to My example in living and, if need be, suffering or perhaps dying because of faith in Me].
AMP.25: For whoever wishes to save his life [in this world] will [eventually] lose it [through death], but whoever loses his life [in this world] for My sake will find it [that is, life with Me for all eternity].
AMP.26: For what will it profit a man if he gains the whole world [wealth, fame, success], but forfeits his soul? Or what will a man give in exchange for his soul?
AMP.27: For the Son of Man is going to come in the glory and majesty of His Father with His angels, and then He will repay each one in accordance with what he has done.
AMP.28: "I assure you and most solemnly say to you, there are some of those standing here who will not taste death before they see the Son of Man coming in His kingdom."

MSG.1: Some Pharisees and Sadducees were on him again, pressing him to prove himself to them.
MSG.2: He told them, "You have a saying that goes, 'Red sky at night, sailor's delight;
MSG.3: red sky at morning, sailors take warning.' You find it easy enough to forecast the weather—why can't you read the signs of the times?
MSG.4: An evil and wanton generation is always wanting signs and wonders. The only sign you'll get is the Jonah sign." Then he turned on his heel and walked away.
MSG.5: On their way to the other side of the lake, the disciples discovered they had forgotten to bring along bread.
MSG.6: In the meantime, Jesus said to them, "Keep a sharp eye out for Pharisee-Sadducee yeast."</>
MSG.7: Thinking he was scolding them for forgetting bread, they discussed in whispers what to do.
MSG.8: Jesus knew what they were doing and said, "Why all these worried whispers about forgetting the bread? Runt believers!
MSG.9: Haven't you caught on yet? Don't you remember the five loaves of bread and the five thousand people, and how many baskets of fragments you picked up?
MSG.10: Or the seven loaves that fed four thousand, and how many baskets of leftovers you collected?
MSG.11: Haven't you realized yet that bread isn't the problem? The problem is yeast, Pharisee-Sadducee yeast."
MSG.12: Then they got it: that he wasn't concerned about eating, but teaching—the Pharisee-Sadducee kind of teaching.
MSG.13: When Jesus arrived in the villages of Caesarea Philippi, he asked his disciples, "What are people saying about who the Son of Man is?"
MSG.14: They replied, "Some think he is John the Baptizer, some say Elijah, some Jeremiah or one of the other prophets."
MSG.15: He pressed them, "And how about you? Who do you say I am?"
MSG.16: Simon Peter said, "You're the Christ, the Messiah, the Son of the living God."
MSG.17: Jesus came back, "God bless you, Simon, son of Jonah! You didn't get that answer out of books or from teachers. My Father in heaven, God himself, let you in on this secret of who I really am.
MSG.18: And now I'm going to tell you who you are, really are. You are Peter, a rock. This is the rock on which I will put together my church, a church so expansive with energy that not even the gates of hell will be able to keep it out.
MSG.19: "And that's not all. You will have complete and free access to God's kingdom, keys to open any and every door: no more barriers between heaven and earth, earth and heaven. A yes on earth is yes in heaven. A no on earth is no in heaven."
MSG.20: He swore the disciples to secrecy. He made them promise they would tell no one that he was the Messiah.
MSG.21: Then Jesus made it clear to his disciples that it was now necessary for him to go to Jerusalem, submit to an ordeal of suffering at the hands of the religious leaders, be killed, and then on the third day be raised up alive.
MSG.22: Peter took him in hand, protesting, "Impossible, Master! That can never be!"
MSG.23: But Jesus didn't swerve. "Peter, get out of my way. Satan, get lost. You have no idea how God works."
MSG.24: Then Jesus went to work on his disciples. "Anyone who intends to come with me has to let me lead. You're not in the driver's seat; I am. Don't run from suffering; embrace it. Follow me and I'll show you how.
MSG.25: Self-help is no help at all. Self-sacrifice is the way, my way, to finding yourself, your true self.
MSG.26: What kind of deal is it to get everything you want but lose yourself? What could you ever trade your soul for?
MSG.27: "Don't be in such a hurry to go into business for yourself. Before you know it the Son of Man will arrive with all the splendor of his Father, accompanied by an army of angels. You'll get everything you have coming to you, a personal gift.
MSG.28: This isn't pie in the sky by and by. Some of you standing here are going to see it take place, see the Son of Man in kingdom glory."

NIV.1:
The Demand for a Sign
The Pharisees and Sadducees came to Jesus and tested him by asking him to show them a sign from heaven.
NIV.2: He replied, “When evening comes, you say, ‘It will be fair weather, for the sky is red,’
NIV.3: and in the morning, ‘Today it will be stormy, for the sky is red and overcast.’ You know how to interpret the appearance of the sky, but you cannot interpret the signs of the times.
NIV.4: A wicked and adulterous generation looks for a miraculous sign, but none will be given it except the sign of Jonah.” Jesus then left them and went away.
NIV.5:
The Yeast of the Pharisees and Sadducees
When they went across the lake, the disciples forgot to take bread.
NIV.6: “Be careful,” Jesus said to them. “Be on your guard against the yeast of the Pharisees and Sadducees.”
NIV.7: They discussed this among themselves and said, “It is because we didn’t bring any bread.”
NIV.8: Aware of their discussion, Jesus asked, “You of little faith, why are you talking among yourselves about having no bread?
NIV.9: Do you still not understand? Don’t you remember the five loaves for the five thousand, and how many basketfuls you gathered?
NIV.10: Or the seven loaves for the four thousand, and how many basketfuls you gathered?
NIV.11: How is it you don’t understand that I was not talking to you about bread? But be on your guard against the yeast of the Pharisees and Sadducees.”
NIV.12: Then they understood that he was not telling them to guard against the yeast used in bread, but against the teaching of the Pharisees and Sadducees.
NIV.13:
Peter’s Confession of Christ
When Jesus came to the region of Caesarea Philippi, he asked his disciples, “Who do people say the Son of Man is?”
NIV.14: They replied, “Some say John the Baptist; others say Elijah; and still others, Jeremiah or one of the prophets.”
NIV.15: “But what about you?” he asked. “Who do you say I am?”
NIV.16: Simon Peter answered, “You are the Christ, the Son of the living God.”
NIV.17: Jesus replied, “Blessed are you, Simon son of Jonah, for this was not revealed to you by man, but by my Father in heaven.
NIV.18: And I tell you that you are Peter, and on this rock I will build my church, and the gates of Hades will not overcome it.
NIV.19: I will give you the keys of the kingdom of heaven; whatever you bind on earth will be bound in heaven, and whatever you loose on earth will be loosed in heaven.”
NIV.20: Then he warned his disciples not to tell anyone that he was the Christ.
NIV.21:
Jesus Predicts His Death
From that time on Jesus began to explain to his disciples that he must go to Jerusalem and suffer many things at the hands of the elders, chief priests and teachers of the law, and that he must be killed and on the third day be raised to life.
NIV.22: Peter took him aside and began to rebuke him. “Never, Lord!” he said. “This shall never happen to you!”
NIV.23: Jesus turned and said to Peter, “Get behind me, Satan! You are a stumbling block to me; you do not have in mind the things of God, but the things of men.”
NIV.24: Then Jesus said to his disciples, “If anyone would come after me, he must deny himself and take up his cross and follow me.
NIV.25: For whoever wants to save his life will lose it, but whoever loses his life for me will find it.
NIV.26: What good will it be for a man if he gains the whole world, yet forfeits his soul? Or what can a man give in exchange for his soul?
NIV.27: For the Son of Man is going to come in his Father’s glory with his angels, and then he will reward each person according to what he has done.
NIV.28: I tell you the truth, some who are standing here will not taste death before they see the Son of Man coming in his kingdom.”

NRSVCE.1: The Pharisees and Sadducees came, and to test Jesus they asked him to show them a sign from heaven.
NRSVCE.2: He answered them, “When it is evening, you say, ‘It will be fair weather, for the sky is red.’
NRSVCE.3: And in the morning, ‘It will be stormy today, for the sky is red and threatening.’ You know how to interpret the appearance of the sky, but you cannot interpret the signs of the times.
NRSVCE.4: An evil and adulterous generation asks for a sign, but no sign will be given to it except the sign of Jonah.” Then he left them and went away.
NRSVCE.5: When the disciples reached the other side, they had forgotten to bring any bread.
NRSVCE.6: Jesus said to them, “Watch out, and beware of the yeast of the Pharisees and Sadducees.”
NRSVCE.7: They said to one another, “It is because we have brought no bread.”
NRSVCE.8: And becoming aware of it, Jesus said, “You of little faith, why are you talking about having no bread?
NRSVCE.9: Do you still not perceive? Do you not remember the five loaves for the five thousand, and how many baskets you gathered?
NRSVCE.10: Or the seven loaves for the four thousand, and how many baskets you gathered?
NRSVCE.11: How could you fail to perceive that I was not speaking about bread? Beware of the yeast of the Pharisees and Sadducees!”
NRSVCE.12: Then they understood that he had not told them to beware of the yeast of bread, but of the teaching of the Pharisees and Sadducees.
NRSVCE.13: Now when Jesus came into the district of Caesarea Philippi, he asked his disciples, “Who do people say that the Son of Man is?”
NRSVCE.14: And they said, “Some say John the Baptist, but others Elijah, and still others Jeremiah or one of the prophets.”
NRSVCE.15: He said to them, “But who do you say that I am?”
NRSVCE.16: Simon Peter answered, “You are the Messiah, the Son of the living God.”
NRSVCE.17: And Jesus answered him, “Blessed are you, Simon son of Jonah! For flesh and blood has not revealed this to you, but my Father in heaven.
NRSVCE.18: And I tell you, you are Peter, and on this rock I will build my church, and the gates of Hades will not prevail against it.
NRSVCE.19: I will give you the keys of the kingdom of heaven, and whatever you bind on earth will be bound in heaven, and whatever you loose on earth will be loosed in heaven.”
NRSVCE.20: Then he sternly ordered the disciples not to tell anyone that he was the Messiah.
NRSVCE.21: From that time on, Jesus began to show his disciples that he must go to Jerusalem and undergo great suffering at the hands of the elders and chief priests and scribes, and be killed, and on the third day be raised.
NRSVCE.22: And Peter took him aside and began to rebuke him, saying, “God forbid it, Lord! This must never happen to you.”
NRSVCE.23: But he turned and said to Peter, “Get behind me, Satan! You are a stumbling block to me; for you are setting your mind not on divine things but on human things.”
NRSVCE.24: Then Jesus told his disciples, “If any want to become my followers, let them deny themselves and take up their cross and follow me.
NRSVCE.25: For those who want to save their life will lose it, and those who lose their life for my sake will find it.
NRSVCE.26: For what will it profit them if they gain the whole world but forfeit their life? Or what will they give in return for their life?
NRSVCE.27: “For the Son of Man is to come with his angels in the glory of his Father, and then he will repay everyone for what has been done.
NRSVCE.28: Truly I tell you, there are some standing here who will not taste death before they see the Son of Man coming in his kingdom.”

NET.1: Now when the Pharisees and Sadducees came to test Jesus, they asked him to show them a sign from heaven.
NET.2: He said, “When evening comes you say, ‘It will be fair weather, because the sky is red,’
NET.3: and in the morning, ‘It will be stormy today, because the sky is red and darkening.’ You know how to judge correctly the appearance of the sky, but you cannot evaluate the signs of the times.
NET.4: A wicked and adulterous generation asks for a sign, but no sign will be given to it except the sign of Jonah.” Then he left them and went away.
NET.5: When the disciples went to the other side, they forgot to take bread.
NET.6: “Watch out,” Jesus said to them, “beware of the yeast of the Pharisees and Sadducees.”
NET.7: So they began to discuss this among themselves, saying, “It is because we brought no bread.”
NET.8: When Jesus learned of this, he said, “You who have such little faith! Why are you arguing among yourselves about having no bread?
NET.9: Do you still not understand? Don’t you remember the five loaves for the five thousand, and how many baskets you took up?
NET.10: Or the seven loaves for the four thousand and how many baskets you took up?
NET.11: How could you not understand that I was not speaking to you about bread? But beware of the yeast of the Pharisees and Sadducees!”
NET.12: Then they understood that he had not told them to be on guard against the yeast in bread, but against the teaching of the Pharisees and Sadducees.
NET.13: When Jesus came to the area of Caesarea Philippi, he asked his disciples, “Who do people say that the Son of Man is?”
NET.14: They answered, “Some say John the Baptist, others Elijah, and others Jeremiah or one of the prophets.”
NET.15: He said to them, “But who do you say that I am?”
NET.16: Simon Peter answered, “You are the Christ, the Son of the living God.”
NET.17: And Jesus answered him, “You are blessed, Simon son of Jonah, because flesh and blood did not reveal this to you, but my Father in heaven!
NET.18: And I tell you that you are Peter, and on this rock I will build my church, and the gates of Hades will not overpower it.
NET.19: I will give you the keys of the kingdom of heaven. Whatever you bind on earth will have been bound in heaven, and whatever you release on earth will have been released in heaven.”
NET.20: Then he instructed his disciples not to tell anyone that he was the Christ.
NET.21: From that time on Jesus began to show his disciples that he must go to Jerusalem and suffer many things at the hands of the elders, chief priests, and experts in the law, and be killed, and on the third day be raised.
NET.22: So Peter took him aside and began to rebuke him: “God forbid, Lord! This must not happen to you!”
NET.23: But he turned and said to Peter, “Get behind me, Satan! You are a stumbling block to me, because you are not setting your mind on God’s interests, but on man’s.”
NET.24: Then Jesus said to his disciples, “If anyone wants to become my follower, he must deny himself, take up his cross, and follow me.
NET.25: For whoever wants to save his life will lose it, but whoever loses his life for my sake will find it.
NET.26: For what does it benefit a person if he gains the whole world but forfeits his life? Or what can a person give in exchange for his life?
NET.27: For the Son of Man will come with his angels in the glory of his Father, and then he will reward each person according to what he has done.
NET.28: I tell you the truth, there are some standing here who will not experience death before they see the Son of Man coming in his kingdom.”
